data (Template)

ndim: int

order: int
__init__(data, original_shape, ndim, batch_size, shape, order)
Parameters:

  • data (list | ndarray | Template)

  • ndim (int)

  • batch_size (int)

  • shape (tuple[int, int])

  • order (int)

Return type:

None

class openfhe_numpy.tensor.tensor.FHETensor(data: Template, original_shape: Tuple[int, int], batch_size: int, new_shape: Tuple[int, int], order: int = 0)[source]
class openfhe_numpy.tensor.tensor.FHETensor(info: PackedArrayInformation)

Bases: BaseTensor[Template], Generic[Template]

Concrete base class for tensors in FHE computation.

Parameters:

  • data (TPL) – Underlying encrypted or encoded of a packed encoding array.

  • original_shape (Tuple[int, int]) – Shape before any padding.

  • batch_size (int) – Total number of packed slots.

  • new_shape (Tuple[int, int]) – Since the shape may change after some operations, we need to store the new information.

  • order (int) – Packing order: only support row-major or column-major.

__init__(data, original_shape, batch_size, new_shape, order=0)[source]
Parameters:

  • data (list | ndarray | PackedArrayInformation)

  • original_shape (Tuple[int, int])

  • batch_size (int)

  • new_shape (Tuple[int, int])

  • order (int)

Return type:

None

extra
property size
property dtype
property data: Template

Underlying encrypted/plaintext payload.

property original_shape: Tuple[int, int]

Original shape before any padding was applied.

property shape: Tuple[int, int]

Shape after padding.

property ndim: int

Dimensionality of the original tensor.

property batch_size: int

Total number of packed slots.

property ncols: int

Number of columns after padding

property nrows: int

Number of rows after padding

property order: int

Packing order constant (row-major or column-major).

property is_encrypted: int
property info: Dict[str, Any]

Metadata dict for serialization or inspection.

property T

openfhe_numpy.tensor.tensor.copy_tensor(tensor)[source]

Generic copy constructor for FHETensor and subclasses.

Parameters:

tensor (FHETensor) – Tensor to be copied.

Returns:

A new instance with the same metadata and (optionally deep-copied) data.

Return type:

FHETensor

Constructors

Utilities

Errors

Logging and error handling for OpenFHE-NumPy.

This module provides: 1. A configured logger with consistent log formatting 2. Custom exceptions with stack trace information 3. Convenience functions for logging at different levels 4. Configuration management via environment variables

Environment Variables:

OPENFHE_DEBUG: Enable debug logging (“ON”, “1”, “TRUE”) OPENFHE_LOG_FORMAT: Custom log format string OPENFHE_LOG_FILE: Path to log file (optional) OPENFHE_LOG_MAX_SIZE: Maximum log file size in bytes OPENFHE_LOG_BACKUP_COUNT: Number of backup log files

Usage:

from openfhe_numpy.utils.errors import ONP_DEBUG, ONP_ERROR, ONP_WARNING

ONP_DEBUG(“Processing data…”) ONP_WARNING(“Unusual input detected”) if problem:

ONP_ERROR(“Invalid input data”)

openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.get_config()[source]

Get logging configuration from environment variables with defaults.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.get_logger()[source]

Thread-safe singleton logger with console + optional rotating file output.

Return type:

Logger

exception openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.ONPError(message)[source]

Bases: Exception

Base class for OpenFHE-NumPy errors.

Parameters:

message (str)

__init__(message)[source]
Parameters:

message (str)

exception openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.ONPTypeError(message)[source]

Bases: ONPError, TypeError

Raised when incorrect types are provided.

Parameters:

message (str)

exception openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.ONPDimensionError(message)[source]

Bases: ONPError, ValueError

Raised when an invalid axis is provided.

Parameters:

message (str)

exception openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.ONPValueError(message='Invalid value encountered.')[source]

Bases: ONPError, ValueError

Raised when an invalid value is encountered.

Parameters:

message (str)

__init__(message='Invalid value encountered.')[source]
Parameters:

message (str)

Raised when tensor shapes are incompatible.

message (str)

message (str)

message (str)

message (str)

None

None

None

message (str)

Return type:

None

Parameters:

message (str)

openfhe_numpy.utils.errors.capture_logs(level=logging.DEBUG)[source]

Capture logs for testing.

Returns a handler that collects logs for inspection in tests.

Parameters:

level (int, optional) – Logging level to capture (default: logging.DEBUG)

Returns:

A handler with a ‘messages’ attribute containing captured log messages

Return type:

logging.Handler

Example

handler = capture_logs() ONP_DEBUG(“Test message”) assert “Test message” in handler.messages

Type Checking

openfhe_numpy.utils.typecheck.is_numeric_scalar(x)[source]
Return type:

bool

openfhe_numpy.utils.typecheck.is_numeric_arraylike(x)[source]

Check if x can be converted to a numeric array.

Parameters:

x (Any) – The value to check

Returns:

True if x can be converted to a numeric array, False otherwise

Return type:

bool

Formatting

Matrix Library

openfhe_numpy.utils.matlib.next_power_of_two(x)[source]

Compute the smallest power of two greater than or equal to x.

Parameters:

x (int) – The input number.

Returns:

The next power of two ≥ x.

Return type:

int

openfhe_numpy.utils.matlib.is_power_of_two(x)[source]

Check if a number is a power of two.

Parameters:

x (int) – Number to check.

Returns:

True if x is a power of two, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

openfhe_numpy.utils.matlib.check_single_equality(a, b, eps=EPSILON)[source]

Compare two floating-point values within a tolerance.

Parameters:

  • a (float) – Values to compare.

  • b (float) – Values to compare.

  • eps (float, optional) – Tolerance threshold (default: EPSILON).

Returns:

(is_equal: bool, error: float)

Return type:

tuple

openfhe_numpy.utils.matlib.check_equality_vector(a, b, vector_size=None, eps=EPSILON)[source]

Compare two vectors element-wise, ensuring the maximum absolute error does not exceed eps. The total error is computed using l2 norm sqrt(sum_ (a_i-b_i)^2)

Parameters:

  • a (list) – Input vectors.

  • b (list) – Input vectors.

  • vector_size (int, optional) – Number of entries to compare (default: full length).

  • eps (float) – Comparison tolerance.

Returns:

(is_equal: bool, total_error: float)

Return type:

tuple

openfhe_numpy.utils.matlib.check_equality(a, b, eps=EPSILON)[source]

Compare two matrices element-wise with tolerance.

Parameters:

  • a (list of list) – Matrices to compare.

  • b (list of list) – Matrices to compare.

  • eps (float) – Tolerance threshold.

Returns:

(is_equal: bool, total_error: float)

Return type:

tuple

Packing

Packing utilities for OpenFHE-NumPy.

This module provides functions to pack vectors and matrices into flat arrays with specific layouts (row-wise or column-wise) and padding. These functions are essential for homomorphic encryption operations that require specific data layouts for efficient computation.

The module is organized into sections: 1. Shape and utility functions 2. Vector packing functions 3. Matrix packing functions 4. Conversion functions between different packing formats

openfhe_numpy.utils.packing.reoriginal_shape(vec, batch_size, ncols)[source]

Convert a packed vector back to its original matrix shape.

Parameters:

  • vec (array_like) – The packed vector to reshape into a matrix.

  • batch_size (int) – Total number of slots in the packed vector.

  • ncols (int) – Number of columns in the target matrix.

Returns:

A 2D list representing the reshaped matrix.

Return type:

list

openfhe_numpy.utils.packing.vector_col_major_2_row_major(v, block_size, batch_size)[source]

Convert a column-wise packed vector to row-wise packing.

Parameters:

  • v (array_like) – The column-wise packed vector to convert.

  • block_size (int) – Size of each block in the packed vector.

  • batch_size (int) – Total number of slots in the output vector.

Returns:

The vector repacked in row-wise format.

Return type:

ndarray

openfhe_numpy.utils.packing.vector_row_major_2_col_major(v, block_size, batch_size)[source]

Convert a row-wise packed vector to column-wise packing.

Parameters:

  • v (array_like) – The row-wise packed vector to convert.

  • block_size (int) – Size of each block in the packed vector.

  • batch_size (int) – Total number of slots in the output vector.

Returns:

The vector repacked in column-wise format.

Return type:

ndarray

openfhe_numpy.utils.packing.process_packed_data(data, info)[source]

Reshape a flattened array to its original matrix shape or a new shape.

Parameters:

  • array (array_like) – The flattened array to reshape.

  • info (dictionary) – Array’s metadata

  • data (ndarray)

Returns:

Reshaped array to its desired shape

Return type:

ndarray
